summary: Architecture has meaningful seams: operation plans, process runner, GUI shell helpers, host-environment adapters, runtime config, explicit exports, and package assembly are present and partially covered by Pester. Ship-readiness is not fully proven: the default GUI operation path can choose Terminal mode for env-secret operations, Start-WsusOperation is not exercised end-to-end in automated tests, production WSUS/IIS/SQL/WPF dependencies were not locally available, CI allows unit/GUI test failures, and runtime defaults/module-location logic remains duplicated across scripts/modules.
proven: Module spine and architecture intent are documented in AGENTS.md: main GUI/CLI/monthly maintenance surfaces and module responsibilities at AGENTS.md:5-33; operational flow and external dependencies at AGENTS.md:35-47.
GUI imports a broad module set from a resolved Modules folder and then uses Get-WsusRuntimeConfig when available; evidence: Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:173-219.
Operation planning seam exists and returns Wsus.OperationPlan objects with command/title/mode/timeout/environment; evidence: Modules/WsusOperationPlan.psm1:30-48, 54-83, 86-127, 130-184.
GUI routes install/restore/transfer/maintenance/schedule/cleanup/diagnostics/reset through operation plans and Start-WsusOperation; evidence: Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:2921-3195.
GUI shell seam centralizes secret environment, status text, popup/probe shaping, completion callbacks, and dashboard view model; evidence: Modules/WsusGuiShell.psm1:11-55, 194-253, 319-526.
Host-environment seam normalizes service, SQL, IIS, process command, and path state probes; evidence: Modules/WsusHostEnvironment.psm1:11-99, 103-260.

Build script compiles WsusManagementGui.ps1 with PS2EXE as x64/STA/admin and stages EXE, Scripts, Modules, DomainController, README/logos, and QUICK-START into the zip; evidence: build.ps1:378-461, 463-495.

Architecture/interface tests exercise diagnostic result shape, operation plan construction, secret env plans, repair adapter, runtime config, GUI shell callbacks, dashboard view model, and process host shape; evidence: Tests/WsusArchitectureInterfaces.Tests.ps1:1-260.
Observed baseline subset passed 574 tests with 0 failures and 72 skips on PowerShell 5.1/Pester 5.7.1; evidence: baseline.log:1840-1881.
notProven: Start-WsusOperation end-to-end lifecycle is not automated: runner tests cover exported commands, path resolution, Stop null-safety, Complete callbacks, and parameter validation, while the only process-start lifecycle test is skipped; evidence: Tests/WsusOperationRunner.Tests.ps1:1-291.

Default GUI Terminal mode with environment-secret operation plans is not proven. Install/schedule plans put passwords in Environment and reference $env:WSUS_*; GUI defaults LiveTerminalMode=true and chooses Terminal before plan.Mode; Terminal StartInfo uses UseShellExecute=true. Evidence: Modules/WsusOperationPlan.psm1:86-104, 130-157; Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:95-101, 3130-3184; Modules/WsusOperationRunner.psm1:391-418.

Local production dependencies were absent/not production-proven. Baseline diagnostics show SQL Server, WSUS service, IIS service, WebAdministration module, firewall rules, and content ACLs missing; evidence: baseline.log:520-620.

Package extraction/runtime from dist zip is not automated. Build assembles the zip, and docs require validating the package, but ExeValidation tests only EXE existence/PE/version/parse behavior, not zip contents or extracted startup; evidence: build.ps1:431-495; docs/releases.md:15-33; Tests/ExeValidation.Tests.ps1:62-166.
CI is not a reliable release gate: workflow unit tests and GUI tests use continue-on-error and build step runs build.ps1 -SkipTests -SkipCodeReview; evidence: .github/workflows/gui-tests.yml:29-50.

Runtime defaults are not fully centralized despite Get-WsusRuntimeConfig. C:\WSUS, .\SQLEXPRESS, ports, sqlcmd paths, and module search logic appear in multiple scripts/modules; evidence: Modules/WsusConfig.psm1:600-647; Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:68-74, 247-259; Scripts/Invoke-WsusManagement.ps1:178-234; Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:122-159; Scripts/Install-WsusWithSqlExpress.ps1:133-138, 651-758.

Historical integrated GUI report documents VM-level WSUS/SQL/GUI/import/restore/client-access passes, but sync/catalog completion and client install validation remained blocked/not tested; evidence: Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md:3-21, 61-80, 143-168.

findings.title: Default GUI operation path does not prove env-secret operations work in Terminal mode
findings.evidence: Install and schedule plans place secrets in Environment and reference WSUS_INSTALL_SA_PASSWORD / WSUS_TASK_PASSWORD in the child command (Modules/WsusOperationPlan.psm1:86-104, 130-157). GUI defaults LiveTerminalMode=true (Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:95-101) and chooses Terminal when LiveTerminalMode is true, before considering operationPlan.Mode (Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:3130-3132). Start-WsusOperation adds EnvironmentVariables then Terminal mode sets UseShellExecute=true and a visible console (Modules/WsusOperationRunner.psm1:391-418). Automated tests only assert the Environment and ProcessHost parameters exist, not that a Terminal child receives env values (Tests/WsusOperationRunner.Tests.ps1:238-247).
findings.risk: GUI Install WSUS and Schedule Task may fail in the default mode because the child process may not receive the password environment variables. If a maintainer switches to command-line secrets to compensate, it would violate the repo rule against secrets on command lines.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Force env-bearing plans to Embedded mode, or prove with an automated Windows PowerShell 5.1 test that Terminal-mode Start-WsusOperation passes environment variables to a child process. Add an assertion that generated install/schedule commands never inline plaintext secrets.
findings.recommendedTests: Add targeted Pester that creates an env-bearing operation plan, launches a tiny child command that writes whether WSUS_TEST_SECRET is present, runs both Embedded and Terminal modes where possible, and asserts cleanup removes the env key. Add GUI-level test for install/schedule start preflight through Invoke-LogOperation with LiveTerminalMode=true.

findings.title: Operation runner seam is partially tested; actual start/timeout/output lifecycle is not automated
findings.evidence: Start-WsusOperation owns process creation, event subscriptions, output handlers, stdin timers, and timeout watchdog (Modules/WsusOperationRunner.psm1:286-612). Tests cover Find-WsusScript, Stop null safety, Complete-WsusOperation status/callbacks, and parameter validation (Tests/WsusOperationRunner.Tests.ps1:24-291). The only Start-WsusOperation process lifecycle test is inside a skipped Context (Tests/WsusOperationRunner.Tests.ps1:277-291).
findings.risk: Regressions in stdout/stderr capture, timeout killing, event cleanup, GUI re-enable, or terminal launch can ship undetected even though the public interface exists.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Create deterministic runner tests using the ProcessHost seam or a minimal real child process under Windows PowerShell 5.1. Cover Embedded success/failure, timeout, OnComplete exactly once, event/timer cleanup, and operation-running flag reset.
findings.recommendedTests: Use a fake ProcessHost adapter where possible. For one Windows-only integration test, launch powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command 'Write-Output ok; exit 0' in Embedded mode and assert log append + completion callback; another with Start-Sleep beyond a small timeout should assert kill + failure.

findings.title: Build/package assembly exists, but package runtime is not release-gated
findings.evidence: build.ps1 creates Scripts/Modules/DomainController staging directories and copies .ps1/.psm1 files plus EXE/readme/logos/quickstart into WsusManager-v$Version.zip (build.ps1:431-495). Release docs explicitly require validating dist package contents (docs/releases.md:15-33). ExeValidation tests check only EXE presence, PE header, x64, version, and script parse—not zip contents, companion folders, or extracted launch (Tests/ExeValidation.Tests.ps1:62-166).
findings.risk: A release can contain a valid EXE but miss required companion folders/files, or fail only after extraction. This is specifically high-impact because docs and runtime require Scripts/ and Modules/ beside the EXE.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Add package validation that opens dist/WsusManager-vX.X.X.zip, asserts GA-WsusManager.exe, Scripts/*.ps1, Modules/*.psm1, DomainController/ where expected, README/QUICK-START, and then extracts to a temp path and runs startup probe if WPF/desktop is available.
findings.recommendedTests: Pester package contract test after build; clean-folder startup probe for extracted package; negative test that moving EXE alone produces the expected clear error, not silent failure.

findings.title: CI workflow does not enforce architecture/build quality gates
findings.evidence: The self-hosted workflow runs Invoke-Pester but has continue-on-error: true ( .github/workflows/gui-tests.yml:29-37 ), builds with .\build.ps1 -SkipTests -SkipCodeReview ( .github/workflows/gui-tests.yml:39-41 ), and GUI tests also continue-on-error ( .github/workflows/gui-tests.yml:43-50 ). Local docs say validation/build should run analyzer, tests, compile, package, and copy to dist (docs/ci-cd.md:22-49).
findings.risk: Broken tests, GUI failures, analyzer errors, and packaging regressions can be uploaded as artifacts without failing the workflow. This undermines ship-readiness evidence even if local scripts are capable of blocking failures.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Make unit/analyzer/build/package gates fail the workflow by default. If GUI tests must be advisory, separate them from release gating and require explicit artifact review before release.
findings.recommendedTests: CI should run build/Invoke-LocalValidation.ps1, build.ps1 -NoPush, package contract tests, and only mark release-ready when all required jobs fail on error.

findings.title: Runtime defaults and dependency resolution remain duplicated across surfaces
findings.evidence: Get-WsusRuntimeConfig centralizes SqlInstance, ContentPath, LogPath, ports, tool names, and SQL Express limit (Modules/WsusConfig.psm1:612-647). However GUI still initializes C:\WSUS/.\SQLEXPRESS/log/install defaults directly (Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:68-74), implements its own sqlcmd path search (Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:247-259), management and monthly maintenance scripts implement separate module search/import logic (Scripts/Invoke-WsusManagement.ps1:178-234; Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:122-159), and install script hardcodes C:\WSUS paths/SQL instance/ports (Scripts/Install-WsusWithSqlExpress.ps1:133-138, 651-758).
findings.risk: Changes to install root, SQL instance, ports, log locations, or tool paths can drift across GUI, CLI, maintenance, diagnostics, and installer. Maintainers must update multiple places, increasing regression risk in air-gapped deployments.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Move remaining operational defaults and tool discovery behind WsusConfig/WsusHostEnvironment-style functions, then update scripts to consume those interfaces unless a value is explicitly provided by parameters/user settings.
findings.recommendedTests: Config contract tests should instantiate runtime config and assert GUI/CLI/maintenance/install plan builders use it. Add a non-default ContentPath/SqlInstance test for operation plan generation and module resolution.

findings.title: Production WSUS/IIS/SQL/WPF integration is blocked locally and only historically documented
findings.evidence: Local baseline diagnostics repeatedly reported missing SQL Server, WSUS service, IIS service, WebAdministration module, firewall rules, and content ACLs (baseline.log:520-620). Historical Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01 documents passes for GUI launch, WSUS/SQL install, post-install GUI harness, diagnostics, import, restore, WS01 access, and client scan/reporting (Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md:3-21, 61-80, 150-168), but it also states upstream sync/catalog remained running and client install validation was not tested (Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md:143-168).
findings.risk: Current local review cannot prove production runtime behavior for WSUS role, IIS/WsusPool, SUSDB restore/sync, GroupPolicy/GPMC, WPF desktop, or client update install flow. Architecture may be sound while integrations still fail in real environments.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Run release candidate on a fresh Windows Server VM with WSUS/IIS/SQL Express and a client VM/domain where applicable. Capture install, package extraction startup, GUI operations, import/export, restore, sync completion, approval, client detection, and install evidence.
findings.recommendedTests: Repeat MS01/WS01-style integration with current artifact; include completed upstream sync, approved update visibility, download/install result, GPO flow on a DC or clearly mark GPO out of scope.

findings.title: Build script performs git commit/push by default unless -NoPush is supplied
findings.evidence: build.ps1 documents NoPush as optional (build.ps1:29-37), and after packaging copies outputs into dist then runs git add/commit/push when NoPush is not set (build.ps1:530-581). AGENTS.md also warns default build may commit/push unless -NoPush is used (AGENTS.md:89-96).
findings.risk: Build/package structure is coupled to repository mutation and remote publishing. A maintainer can accidentally publish dist artifacts from a local build or dirty environment, reducing release reproducibility.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Invert the default: never push unless an explicit -Push or release workflow flag is supplied. Keep packaging as a pure build artifact step.
findings.recommendedTests: Add static/script test that default build path does not contain git commit/push unless an explicit publishing switch is true.

findings.title: Architecture documentation inventory is stale relative to module count
findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md and README describe Modules/ as 16 modules (CLAUDE.md:32-54; README.md:347-370), while the workspace contains 25 .psm1 files under Modules/ from the find result. AGENTS.md has a newer module-spine summary but still requires maintainers to reconcile multiple docs.
findings.risk: New maintainers and agents can miss newer seams such as WsusHostEnvironment, WsusProcessHost, WsusOperationPlan, WsusRepairPlan, WsusTestHarness, and WsusDiagnosticResult, increasing parallel-convention risk.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Update docs/wikis/module reference from actual Modules/*.psm1 inventory and mark authoritative architecture docs. Remove stale module-count claims.
findings.recommendedTests: Add documentation lint/check that Module-Reference or README inventory includes every Modules/*.psm1 public export, or generate the inventory from Export-ModuleMember declarations.

featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Partially implemented. WsusOperationPlan, WsusOperationRunner, WsusGuiShell, WsusHostEnvironment, WsusProcessHost, WsusHealth, and others export explicit functions.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Static read plus Pester architecture/interface tests.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: Runner start lifecycle, timeout, and terminal/env behavior not automated.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Add lifecycle tests through ProcessHost and Windows-only smoke coverage.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: Pester for Start-WsusOperation Embedded/Terminal success/failure/timeout/env cleanup.

featureMatrix.expected: GUI selects operation intent, planner builds command/env/timeout, runner owns execution, GUI shell owns UI completion/history/notification cleanup.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Implemented in code and partially covered.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Read GUI Invoke-LogOperation and architecture tests for operation plans and GUI shell completion callbacks.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: Default Terminal mode with env secrets not proven; Start-WsusOperation process lifecycle test skipped.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Force secret operations to Embedded or prove Terminal environment propagation; add operation runner lifecycle tests.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: GUI operation-plan tests for install/schedule/maintenance/diagnostics; runner tests with env-bearing command.

featureMatrix.expected: One source for C:\WSUS, .\SQLEXPRESS, ports, logs, tools, timeouts; scripts consume config rather than duplicating assumptions.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Partially implemented via Get-WsusRuntimeConfig and Get-WsusOperationTimeout, but duplicated defaults remain in multiple scripts/modules.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Static search/read for hard-coded defaults and config usage.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: Multiple sqlcmd discovery paths, module search paths, and default content/SQL/port declarations.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Move remaining defaults/tool discovery into WsusConfig/WsusHostEnvironment and consume from all entry points.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: Non-default config tests across GUI plan generation, CLI runtime initialization, maintenance, and installer.

featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Route remaining direct host calls through adapters where tests need determinism; run on production-like VM for proof.

featureMatrix.expected: Repeatable PS2EXE build, required companion folders in zip, package validation, no accidental publishing.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Build script compiles and packages required folders; release docs specify validation; default build can git commit/push unless NoPush; package validation not automated.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Read build.ps1, docs, ExeValidation tests.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: No zip content/extracted-startup test; build side-effects by default.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Add package contract/extraction tests and make publishing explicit opt-in.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: Post-build Pester over dist zip, clean extraction startup probe, verify Scripts/Modules/DomainController inventory.

featureMatrix.testedHow: Read workflow, build script, local validation script, baseline log.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: CI does not enforce the same gates docs require; GUI test failures are advisory.

featureMatrix.expected: Architecture claims backed by current execution on Windows Server/WSUS/IIS/SQL/WPF and client/domain flows, or explicitly blocked.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Blocked locally. Historical report documents substantial VM pass but not current local execution and not complete sync/install coverage.

featureMatrix.remainingGaps: Current local machine lacks WSUS/IIS/SQL stack; sync/catalog completion and client install validation not proven.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Run current artifact in production-like VM/client/DC environment and capture install/sync/import/restore/GPO/client-install evidence.

featureMatrix.evidence: AGENTS.md:5-96; CLAUDE.md:32-54; README.md:347-370; docs/releases.md:5-33; find result for Modules/*.psm1 returned 25 files.

summary: Security controls are partially present, but credential exposure paths, broad firewall profile rules, and unproven domain-wide GPO actions block production ship readiness. Review was static/code-evidence only for WSUS/IIS/SQL/GPO runtime paths; those require production-like validation on an elevated WSUS server/domain controller.
proven: GUI operation plans avoid embedding install and scheduled-task passwords directly in the PowerShell command string by using WSUS_INSTALL_SA_PASSWORD / WSUS_TASK_PASSWORD environment variables: Modules/WsusOperationPlan.psm1:95-102,153-157.
GUI path gates reject common shell metacharacters before install/restore/transfer operations: Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:262-268,3012-3071.
Restore backup resolution requires existing .bak files before restore operations: Modules/WsusProvisioning.psm1:73-113 and Scripts/Invoke-WsusManagement.ps1:497-555.

Admin enforcement exists for Invoke-WsusManagement.ps1, Invoke-WsusClientCheckIn.ps1, Set-WsusHttps.ps1, and DomainController/Set-WsusGroupPolicy.ps1 via #Requires -RunAsAdministrator or Test-AdminPrivileges.
Some SQL restore paths escape single quotes before composing RESTORE DATABASE SQL: Scripts/Invoke-WsusManagement.ps1:552-555 and Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:563-588.
notProven: No production-like WSUS/IIS/SQL Express/GPMC execution was available locally, so SQL restore/sync, wsusutil, IIS HTTPS binding, scheduled task creation, firewall rule application, and domain GPO deployment are Blocked pending a real elevated lab run.

Command-injection resistance is only partially proven: operation plans build powershell.exe -Command strings (Modules/WsusOperationRunner.psm1:392-418); existing tests assert string presence, not adversarial path/product/user inputs.
Log/privacy safety is not proven: transcript logging and GUI log capture were read statically, but no redaction test verifies passwords, SQL errors, command lines, or GPO host lists are excluded.

GPO/domain safety is not production-proven: helper tests cover OU naming and link deletion, but not a real domain import, backup contents, blast-radius confirmation, rollback, or gpupdate push behavior.

findings.title: Remove plaintext SA password CLI flow
findings.evidence: Install-WsusWithSqlExpress.ps1 exposes [string]$SaPassword as a documented plain-text parameter at lines 28-31, starts a transcript to C:\WSUS\Logs\install.log at lines 166-167, converts that password from plain text and stores it at lines 349-350, and writes SAPWD="$SA_Password" into ConfigurationFile.ini at lines 395-420 before scrubbing only after setup at lines 436-443.
findings.risk: The SA password can be exposed through process command lines, transcript headers/host metadata, and the SQL setup configuration file while setup runs or if the host/script terminates before cleanup.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Remove -SaPassword plain-text input; accept a SecureString prompt or a protected one-time secret source, avoid transcript capture around secret handling, ACL any transient setup file to Administrators/SYSTEM only, and prove cleanup on success/failure/cancel.
findings.recommendedTests: Add tests that operation commands and transcript/log output never contain the SA value; run an install failure/cancel scenario and assert ConfigurationFile.ini is absent or has SAPWD blank with restrictive ACLs.

findings.title: Avoid passing SQL credentials to sqlcmd.exe with -P
findings.evidence: Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1 uses stored SQL credentials in unattended mode at lines 227-238. If Invoke-Sqlcmd is unavailable, Invoke-WsusSqlcmd falls back to sqlcmd.exe and appends -U plus -P with Credential.GetNetworkCredential().Password at Modules/WsusUtilities.psm1:521-545.
findings.risk: The SQL password is visible in the sqlcmd.exe process command line to local administrators, EDR/process collectors, and diagnostic dumps during unattended maintenance.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Do not support SQL-auth fallback through sqlcmd -P. Require the SqlServer module for PSCredential use, use Windows Integrated auth for scheduled tasks, or use a non-command-line secret mechanism; fail closed if only sqlcmd.exe is available with a credential.
findings.recommendedTests: Mock Invoke-Sqlcmd unavailable + sqlcmd present and assert Invoke-WsusSqlcmd rejects PSCredential without spawning sqlcmd or including the password in any argument list/log.

findings.title: Narrow WSUS firewall rules off Public profile
findings.evidence: Modules/WsusFirewall.psm1 defines inbound WSUS HTTP 8530 and HTTPS 8531 allow rules with Profile = "Domain,Private,Public" at lines 21-39, and Initialize-WsusFirewallRules applies those definitions at lines 200-224. SQL rules are narrower (Domain,Private) at lines 42-59.
findings.risk: If the server is ever on a Public network profile, WSUS ports are opened to that network even though this tool is for managed/domain WSUS administration.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Limit WSUS inbound rules to Domain (or Domain,Private only with explicit operator opt-in) and document any Public-profile requirement with a production firewall justification.
findings.recommendedTests: Unit-test the firewall rule definitions and a mocked New-NetFirewallRule call to assert WSUS profiles exclude Public by default.

findings.title: Add confirmation and scoping for domain-wide GPO push
findings.evidence: DomainController/Set-WsusGroupPolicy.ps1 links WSUS Update Policy to the domain root at lines 337-346, then Invoke-WsusGroupPolicyImport unconditionally calls Push-GPUpdateToAll at lines 608-626. Push-GPUpdateToAll enumerates all enabled domain computers excluding DCs and creates/runs a SYSTEM scheduled task remotely via schtasks.exe at lines 424-491.
findings.risk: A single script run can immediately force gpupdate across the domain after replacing/importing GPOs, with no explicit confirmation, allowlist, dry-run, or rollback checkpoint in the code path.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Gate Push-GPUpdateToAll behind an explicit switch plus confirmation, support an OU/computer allowlist and -WhatIf, and record rollback/backup IDs before changing links.
findings.recommendedTests: Mock AD/GPO/schtasks calls and verify default execution imports GPOs without pushing, while -PushGpUpdate scopes only requested targets and reports failures without continuing silently.

findings.title: Enforce administrator preflight for maintenance
findings.evidence: Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1 notes it must run as Administrator at lines 67-70 but has no #Requires -RunAsAdministrator and no Test-AdminPrivileges call near param/startup lines 75-78 or before WSUS/SQL/service operations beginning at lines 620-715.
findings.risk: Non-elevated execution can reach WSUS/SQL/service/export phases and fail partway through, producing partial maintenance state or misleading logs instead of a clear admin-only denial.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Add #Requires -RunAsAdministrator or an early Test-AdminPrivileges -ExitOnFail guard before logging and preflight side effects; prove scheduled-task execution runs under an admin/service account.
findings.recommendedTests: Add a mocked non-admin startup test that asserts the script exits before WSUS connection, sync, backup, export, or service mutations are attempted.

featureMatrix.expected: All user-controlled paths, product names, usernames, URLs, and operation IDs are passed as arguments or safely quoted before reaching powershell.exe, robocopy, wsusutil, sqlcmd, schtasks, and Start-Process.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Partially implemented. GUI Test-SafePath rejects metacharacters and ConvertTo-WsusCommandLiteral escapes single quotes, but operation execution still composes powershell.exe -Command strings and several native command calls use free-form strings/arrays.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Static read of Scripts/*.ps1, Modules/WsusOperationPlan.psm1, Modules/WsusOperationRunner.psm1, and existing tests. No adversarial command-injection tests observed.
featureMatrix.evidence: Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:262-268; Modules/WsusOperationPlan.psm1:11-15,70-81,115-124,153-171; Modules/WsusOperationRunner.psm1:392-418; Tests/Integration.Tests.ps1:144-179 only checks string/function presence.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: Need adversarial tests for paths/products/users containing quotes, semicolons, PowerShell metacharacters, UNC edge cases, and GPO URLs; prefer ProcessStartInfo argument lists over -Command strings where possible.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Add tests and refactor high-risk command construction to argument arrays or encoded script blocks with explicit parameters.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: Pester tests for each operation plan plus mocked Start-Process/ProcessStartInfo assertions that malicious input is data, not syntax.

featureMatrix.expected: SQL statements built from file paths, instance names, usernames, and maintenance values are parameterized or constrained/escaped consistently.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Partially implemented. Restore/backup verification paths escape single quotes or validate .bak format; some SQL statements are constant. Maintenance backup SQL interpolates a generated path from config without local escaping but default path is controlled.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Static read; no live SQL or injection tests run.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: Need tests proving every interpolated SQL path/user/value is escaped or constrained, especially backup/export content paths and sqlcmd variable substitution.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Centralize SQL literal escaping/validation for file paths and forbid ad-hoc SQL interpolation outside that helper.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: Mock Invoke-WsusSqlcmd and assert generated SQL for paths containing apostrophes remains a single string literal; add negative tests for invalid paths.

featureMatrix.expected: No passwords in command lines, process args, transcripts, broad environment variables, or world-readable files; transient secrets are cleared and failure-safe.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Failed for CLI/install and sqlcmd fallback; partially mitigated for GUI operation plans through child environment variables.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Static read of credential and operation execution paths; no process-list/log redaction test observed.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: Plaintext SA CLI parameter, SAPWD setup file window, sqlcmd -P fallback, and environment-variable secret exposure during child process lifetime.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Remove plaintext secret channels, fail closed on unsafe fallbacks, restrict transient files, and prove logs/process args never include secrets.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: Secret canary tests across GUI/CLI install, scheduled-task creation, unattended maintenance, transcript files, and mocked process argument capture.

featureMatrix.feature: Path validation and unsafe file operations
featureMatrix.expected: Destructive copy/remove/restore paths are canonicalized, type-checked, scoped, and reject traversal/metacharacters before use.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Partially implemented. GUI and CLI have path checks; restore helper requires .bak. Some utility path creation/removal helpers are thin wrappers, and production destructive paths were not run.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Static read and existing tests for helper availability/resolution; no destructive E2E on real paths.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: Need canonicalization tests for symlinks/reparse points, UNC/admin shares, non-existent destinations, and delete/rename operations such as client cache and history/trend cleanup.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Use Resolve-Path/GetFullPath where possible, validate PathType for all destructive operations, and add explicit scoping for C:\WSUS/app-data only where intended.

summary: Static audit found real build/package/install scaffolding, but runtime-deployment is not ship-ready: CI is non-gating/stale, clean WSUS+SQL+IIS deployment is not production-proven locally, release/package docs conflict with build behavior, and rollback/recovery is incomplete for destructive install paths.
proven: build.ps1 has a PS2EXE-based build path that targets Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1, emits GA-WsusManager.exe, requires admin, STA, x64, and version 4.0.5 (build.ps1:40-57, build.ps1:362-412).
build.ps1 stages a distribution zip with GA-WsusManager.exe, Scripts/*.ps1, Modules/*.psm1, DomainController/*, README.md, branding assets, and a generated QUICK-START.txt (build.ps1:428-520).
docs/releases.md documents manual pre-release, build output, GitHub release, and post-release validation checklist (docs/releases.md:13-48).

README.md and docs/QUICK-START.md document operator install paths, required admin privileges, SQL installer location, clean install flow, air-gap restore flow, GPO deployment, and standard paths (README.md:45-163, README.md:405-419, docs/QUICK-START.md:1-119).

Local logging/storage locations are present in code: GUI daily log C:\WSUS\Logs\WsusOperations_yyyy-MM-dd.log, settings under %APPDATA%\WsusManager\settings.json, module log defaults, and history JSON recovery behavior (Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:60-112, Modules/WsusUtilities.psm1:149-218, Modules/WsusHistory.psm1:19-72).

There are tests for EXE file/PE metadata if an EXE already exists, startup probe on Windows, GUI startup/navigation prerequisites, and installer parameter shape; these are evidence of some deployability test intent, not production proof (Tests/ExeValidation.Tests.ps1:1-213, Tests/StartupE2E.Tests.ps1:27-66, Tests/FlaUI.Tests.ps1:1-27, Tests/CliIntegration.Tests.ps1:181-230).
notProven: Clean install from release zip on Windows Server with real SQL Express media, WSUS role, IIS, wsusutil.exe, firewall, and permissions is Blocked by missing production-like stack locally; proof needed: clean Server 2019/2022 VM run as Administrator using WsusManager-v4.0.5.zip plus SQL media, then verify SQL, IIS, WSUS services, IIS /Content, firewall, C:\WSUS ACLs, dashboard, diagnostics, and one WSUS operation.
Release asset integrity is Not Proven; no observed build.ps1 -NoPush run, no observed dist zip inspection, no package manifest/hash/signing evidence, and no clean-folder launch evidence.
CI/CD quality gate is Not Proven; only .github/workflows/gui-tests.yml exists, and it is self-hosted/manual/scheduled with continue-on-error on tests and build step skipping tests and code review.

Runtime WSUS operations, SQL restore/sync, GroupPolicy/GPMC deployment, WPF desktop E2E, and source-server export are Blocked locally by missing WSUS/IIS/SQL Express/GPMC/interactive desktop production dependencies.

Rollback/recovery for failed install/migration is Not Proven; docs cover DB restore/reset but not full product install rollback, WID migration rollback, package rollback, or release rollback execution.

findings.title: CI is non-gating and contradicts release documentation
findings.evidence: Only .github/workflows/gui-tests.yml was found; .github/workflows/build.yml is absent. The GUI workflow runs on a specific self-hosted runner, has no push/pull_request trigger, runs unit tests with broad excluded tags and continue-on-error, builds with .\build.ps1 -SkipTests -SkipCodeReview, and runs GUI tests with continue-on-error (.github/workflows/gui-tests.yml:1-58). README claims a missing .github/workflows/build.yml runs analyzer, Pester, PS2EXE, EXE validation, and startup benchmarks on every push/PR (README.md:380-401). docs/ci-cd.md says GitHub Actions should mirror local commands if added back later, implying local validation is source of truth (docs/ci-cd.md:1-35).
findings.risk: A failing test suite, failed GUI automation, stale code analysis, or missing release workflow can still produce/upload artifacts. The README overstates automated release protection.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Add a gating push/PR workflow or correct docs to the actual manual-only process. Required gate: analyzer, Pester subset with explicit excludes justified, build.ps1 -NoPush without SkipTests/SkipCodeReview, EXE validation, package extraction/manifest check, and artifact upload only after success. Remove continue-on-error from gating test steps.
findings.recommendedTests: Run the workflow on a clean self-hosted Windows runner and capture a failed-test case proving the workflow fails the check; run a passing case proving artifacts are uploaded only after gates pass.

findings.title: Build script has release side effects and unpinned external dependencies
findings.evidence: build.ps1 auto-installs PSScriptAnalyzer, Pester, and ps2exe from PowerShell Gallery/OneDrive fallback when missing, with no pinned versions (build.ps1:67-83, build.ps1:232-239, build.ps1:301-362). If PSScriptAnalyzer/Pester install fails, build.ps1 can skip code review/tests; warnings do not block (build.ps1:75-83, build.ps1:200-222). Default build commits and pushes dist unless -NoPush is supplied (build.ps1:538-592). Local validation treats missing PSScriptAnalyzer/Pester as warnings and exits 0 on warnings (build/Invoke-LocalValidation.ps1:80-127, build/Invoke-LocalValidation.ps1:184-250).
findings.risk: Builds are not reproducible offline, dependency versions can drift, quality checks can be silently absent, and a normal build can mutate/push release artifacts unexpectedly.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Separate build from publish. Make dependency installation explicit/preflighted and version-pinned. In CI/release mode, fail if tools are missing or checks are skipped. Make -NoPush the safe default or move commit/push to a separate release command.
findings.recommendedTests: Run build on a clean Windows build image with preinstalled pinned modules and no network. Verify build fails when Pester/PSScriptAnalyzer/ps2exe are missing in release mode, and verify build.ps1 -NoPush cannot run git operations.

findings.title: Package and operator docs are inconsistent/stale
findings.evidence: build.ps1 defaults to GA-WsusManager.exe and packages that name (build.ps1:40-57, build.ps1:441-509). README Quick Start tells users to run WsusManager.exe, and README Build/Test says dist output is WsusManager.exe (README.md:45-53, README.md:380-401). docs/QUICK-START.md title/footer say v4.0.4 while package rows say WsusManager-v4.0.5.zip (docs/QUICK-START.md:1-13, docs/QUICK-START.md:119-123). docs/ci-cd.md says release documentation files are copied by build.ps1, but build.ps1 copies README.md and generates QUICK-START.txt; it does not copy docs/releases.md or docs/ci-cd.md (docs/ci-cd.md:43-48, build.ps1:441-509).
findings.risk: Operators may launch the wrong executable name, release notes/version may be distrusted, and package contents may not match the documented release contract.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Normalize executable naming across README, docs/QUICK-START.md, docs/releases.md, build.ps1, tests, and package. Add a generated package manifest and test that extracted zip contains exactly the documented files and current version.
findings.recommendedTests: Build package, extract to a clean temp folder, assert GA-WsusManager.exe/Scripts/Modules/DomainController/docs presence, assert version strings match, and assert README Quick Start command names the actual EXE.

findings.title: Clean WSUS+SQL+IIS deployment remains Blocked/Not Proven
findings.evidence: Operator docs require Windows Server 2019/2022, SQL Express installer under C:\WSUS\SQLDB, local admin, then Install WSUS and dashboard green status (docs/QUICK-START.md:6-27; README.md:56-126). Install script depends on SQL media, may download/extract SQL Express, configures SQL networking, handles WID-to-SQL migration by uninstalling WSUS and deleting WID DB files, then installs UpdateServices-Services/DB/UI via Windows Server feature APIs (Scripts/Install-WsusWithSqlExpress.ps1:25-100, Scripts/Install-WsusWithSqlExpress.ps1:252-283, Scripts/Install-WsusWithSqlExpress.ps1:550-610). Existing tests only validate parameter presence/installer resolution shape, not a real install (Tests/CliIntegration.Tests.ps1:181-230, Tests/WsusProvisioning.Tests.ps1:1-35).
findings.risk: The highest-risk deployment path modifies Windows features, SQL networking, IIS/WSUS state, permissions, firewall, and potentially WID data, but no local evidence proves it succeeds from a clean server or recovers from partial failure.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Run a production-like clean install validation on disposable Windows Server 2019/2022: release zip extracted under C:\WSUS, offline SQL media present, run GUI as admin, install WSUS, verify services, SQL instance/SUSDB, wsusutil postinstall, IIS /Content, firewall ports, ACLs, logs, dashboard, diagnostics, and one safe WSUS operation.
findings.recommendedTests: Automate a smoke script that records Get-WindowsFeature, Get-Service MSSQL$SQLEXPRESS/WSUSService/W3SVC, sqlcmd SUSDB query, IIS virtual directory path, Test-NetConnection 8530/8531, C:\WSUS ACL checks, and diagnostics output after install.

findings.title: Rollback/recovery is incomplete for destructive install and release failure modes
findings.evidence: Docs and GUI expose backup/restore/reset flows for SUSDB/content (README.md:79-92, README.md:135-155, docs/QUICK-START.md:37-46). Restore warns operators to create a backup first in the GUI (Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:1990-2013). Install script WID migration stops services, uninstalls WSUS, removes WID SUSDB files, and only warns that reboot may be required (Scripts/Install-WsusWithSqlExpress.ps1:550-590). Release docs post-release checklist validates launch and one operation but has no rollback procedure (docs/releases.md:42-48).
findings.risk: If install/migration fails mid-flight, operators lack a documented reversal path for WSUS feature state, WID DB files, SQL Express state, IIS bindings, firewall/ACL changes, or release package downgrade.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Add a rollback runbook and preflight backup/snapshot requirements before destructive install/migration. For release rollback, document previous asset retention, config/history compatibility, and package downgrade procedure. Prove on a disposable VM by inducing a failed install and restoring service/database/content state.
findings.recommendedTests: Run WID-to-SQL migration test with VM snapshot, force a failure after WSUS uninstall, execute rollback, and verify WID/WSUS or previous SQL-backed WSUS returns healthy. Add restore tests using a known-good SUSDB backup on a disposable WSUS instance.

findings.title: Monitoring/logging is local-only and write failures are largely silent
findings.evidence: README lists dashboard health, database trending, local logs, settings/history/trending files (README.md:71-105, README.md:405-419). GUI Write-Log creates C:\WSUS\Logs and Add-Content's messages, but catches and discards failures (Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:60-112, Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:200-234). WsusUtilities Start-WsusLogging uses Start-Transcript -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue (Modules/WsusUtilities.psm1:149-218). History corrupt JSON is backed up/reset and write failures are warnings/not throwing (Modules/WsusHistory.psm1:19-72, Modules/WsusHistory.psm1:109-112).
findings.risk: For an admin tool this may be acceptable operationally, but production readiness is not proven for log loss, permissions-denied log paths, central monitoring, alerting, or evidence retention after failures.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: At minimum, surface log initialization failures in the GUI/status and document where operators collect logs. For production operations, define whether Windows Event Log, SIEM forwarding, or scheduled health-report output is required.
findings.recommendedTests: Run as admin with C:\WSUS\Logs unavailable/ACL-denied and verify the GUI visibly reports logging failure. Run long maintenance and diagnostics and verify expected log, history, and trend files are written and recover after corrupt JSON.

featureMatrix.expected: Fresh Windows Server install from release zip with SQL Express media, WSUS role, IIS content, SQL networking, firewall, permissions, and dashboard green verification.

featureMatrix.remainingGaps: Needs disposable Server 2019/2022 VM proof with SQL Express media, WSUS/IIS/SQL services, wsusutil, dashboard, diagnostics, and safe operation.

featureMatrix.remainingGaps: No observed clean build; no pinned dependency lock; quality checks are bypassable/permissive; publish coupled to build.

featureMatrix.testedHow: Static inspection of docs and workflow presence.

featureMatrix.testedHow: find/search/read of .github/workflows and docs.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: No push/PR build workflow, no gating unit/GUI failure behavior, no proven artifact validation.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Add gating workflow or explicitly require signed local validation evidence before release.

featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Dashboard health/trending/history/log files exist; logging failures are mostly silent and central monitoring is not defined.

summary: Baseline Pester evidence is real for the mock-heavy/local subset: baseline.log records 646 discovered, 574 passed, 0 failed, 72 skipped on PowerShell 5.1/Pester 5.7.1. Ship readiness is not fully proven because CI tolerates failed unit/GUI jobs, FlaUI/E2E is conditional and skipped without missing harness/build/desktop prerequisites, and WSUS/IIS/SQL/GroupPolicy/client-update flows are mostly mocked or only covered by a manual VM report with explicit remaining gaps.
proven: Local baseline subset passed: baseline.log:1809-1849 shows 574 passed, 0 failed, 72 skipped, 646 total, Pester 5.7.1, PS 5.1.
Syntax/module-loading coverage exists for GUI/CLI scripts and 20 modules: Tests/Integration.Tests.ps1:21-142.

Unit coverage exists for config, product filters, service wrappers, SQL wrapper behavior, export wrapper behavior, operation runner, dialogs, history, permissions, firewall, scheduled task, provisioning, architecture interfaces, and test harness helpers.
Startup probe test can launch Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1 with -E2EStartupProbe and assert a JSON result/no error popups on Windows hosts: Tests/StartupE2E.Tests.ps1:27-89.

A manual integrated VM report claims GUI launch/navigation, full WSUS+SQL install, import/restore, diagnostics, WS01 reachability, scan/reporting, and assigned-update visibility passed in MS01/WS01: Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md:1-129 and :164-183.
notProven: Automated CI does not prove failures block merge: .github/workflows/gui-tests.yml:32-57 marks both unit and GUI test steps continue-on-error, and build step runs .\build.ps1 -SkipTests -SkipCodeReview.

FlaUI GUI automation was not proven locally: baseline.log:1-7 says FlaUI tests skipped because harness not found/assemblies failed to load; repository lookup found no Tests/FlaUITestHarness/**; Tests/FlaUI.Tests.ps1:49-60 skips all describes when harness/exe/assemblies are missing.
Compiled EXE behavior is conditional: Tests/ExeValidation.Tests.ps1:62-148 skips executable validation and startup benchmark when no WsusManager.exe/GA-WsusManager.exe exists.

SQL/WSUS/IIS production integration is not proven by Pester: WsusDatabase tests explicitly mock SQL (Tests/WsusDatabase.Tests.ps1:1-14,61-84); WsusHealth deep diagnostics mock path/IIS/service/SQL/wsusutil/event sources (Tests/WsusHealth.Tests.ps1:313-337); baseline logs expected missing IIS/WSUS/SQL warnings (baseline.log:28-76).

Air-gapped restore/import from a real external upstream export and full WS01 download/install of an approved update remain unproven; the VM report lists these as not yet proven: Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md:429-447.

Monthly maintenance online sync/cleanup/backup/export is not executed in automated tests; CLI tests parse parameters and content rather than run the script through WSUS APIs: Tests/CliIntegration.Tests.ps1:24-207,294-343.
GroupPolicy/DC behavior is unit-tested by replacing AD/GPMC functions, not by running against a domain controller/GPMC stack: Tests/WsusGroupPolicy.Tests.ps1:1-9,64-87.

findings.title: CI test jobs are non-blocking
findings.evidence: .github/workflows/gui-tests.yml:32-57 runs unit tests with -ExcludeTag for many GUI/E2E tags, then sets continue-on-error: true; the GUI scheduled-task step also has continue-on-error: true. The build step uses .\build.ps1 -SkipTests -SkipCodeReview at lines 39-43.
findings.risk: A failing Pester or FlaUI suite can still produce a green workflow artifact path, so CI cannot be used as release evidence.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Remove continue-on-error for unit and GUI steps; make Run-GuiTests.ps1 exit non-zero when Invoke-Pester FailedCount > 0; build without -SkipTests for release validation or add a separate required release gate.
findings.recommendedTests: Add a deliberate failing smoke test on a temporary branch and verify the workflow fails; add CI assertion that UnitTests.xml and flaui-test-results contain zero failed tests.

findings.title: GUI automation is conditional and currently skipped without harness/build prerequisites
findings.evidence: Tests/FlaUI.Tests.ps1:49-60 sets CanRunTests only when Tests\FlaUITestHarness exists, assemblies load, and an EXE/script exists, then skips all GUI describes otherwise. baseline.log:1-7 records 'FlaUI tests skipped: FlaUI harness not found, FlaUI assemblies failed to load' and 72 skipped tests overall. Tests/ExeValidation.Tests.ps1:62-148 also skips executable validation when no EXE exists.
findings.risk: WPF startup/navigation/dialog/button behavior is not repeatably proven on the local ship gate; regressions can hide behind skipped tests.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Check in or provision FlaUI harness/packages in CI, build GA-WsusManager.exe before GUI tests, require interactive admin desktop/session, and fail if any GUI describe is skipped unexpectedly.
findings.recommendedTests: Required GUI gate: Startup, Navigation, Settings, About, History, Help, Performance, Resilience, Cleanup, WSUS-button state, keyboard shortcuts, plus screenshot artifacts on failure.

findings.title: Production WSUS/SQL/IIS flows are mostly mocked in Pester
findings.evidence: Tests/WsusDatabase.Tests.ps1:1-14 states database tests mock to avoid actual database operations; Tests/WsusDatabase.Tests.ps1:61-84 mocks Invoke-WsusSqlcmd success/error. Tests/WsusHealth.Tests.ps1:196-214 mocks repair services/firewall/permissions/IIS path, and :313-337 mocks Test-Path, WebAdministration import, service state, SQL query, wsusutil command, and event logs. Tests/WsusFirewall.Tests.ps1:1-14 and Tests/WsusScheduledTask.Tests.ps1:1-14 state firewall/task modifications are mocked.
findings.risk: The code paths most likely to fail in production—SQL connectivity, WebAdministration/IIS, wsusutil, firewall, scheduled task registration, service control—are only proven against fake adapters in automated tests.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Add an integration lane on a disposable Windows VM with WSUS role, SQL Express/SUSDB, IIS/WebAdministration, wsusutil.exe, and admin rights; tag it separately but make it required for release candidates.
findings.recommendedTests: Run Test-WsusDatabaseConnection, Invoke-WsusDeepDiagnostics, Repair-WsusHealth -AutoFix, New/Start/Remove-WsusMaintenanceTask, firewall create/remove, wsusutil reset/checkhealth, and content-permission repair against real host state, then assert resulting services/IIS/SQL/task/firewall state.

findings.title: Critical CLI workflows are parsed, not executed, by automated tests
findings.evidence: Tests/CliIntegration.Tests.ps1:24-207 parses script ASTs to verify parameters for monthly maintenance, management, and install scripts. Tests/CliIntegration.Tests.ps1:261-343 and Tests/ProductFilter.Tests.ps1:24-285 use raw content/regex simulations for help text, classifications, product filters, and SQL-safety patterns rather than invoking end-to-end operations.
findings.risk: Router/parameter binding, runtime imports, environment variables, process exit codes, transcript/log creation, and operation side effects can break while AST/content tests still pass.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Add non-destructive CLI integration tests that invoke scripts with safe modes/fixtures and assert exit codes, logs, JSON/result objects, and dispatch behavior.
findings.recommendedTests: Invoke-WsusManagement.ps1 -Health/-Diagnostics/-DeepDiagnostics/-Import/-Restore with temp fixtures or VM-backed services; Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1 -Operations SyncOnly/Cleanup/Backup/Export in a staged WSUS VM; Install-WsusWithSqlExpress.ps1 -NonInteractive in disposable VM.

findings.title: Some tests depend on ambient host state instead of isolated seams
findings.evidence: Tests/WsusServices.Tests.ps1:49-76 uses real Spooler/W32Time and a real non-existent service. Tests/WsusAutoDetection.Tests.ps1:70-101,144-258 calls service/database/certificate/disk/overall health functions without mocks. baseline.log:28-76 shows local missing IIS/WSUS/SQL/firewall/permission warnings during test execution.
findings.risk: Results vary by workstation role, installed services, disk layout, permissions, and PowerShell modules; this creates noisy false confidence and can make failures hard to triage.

summary: GUI startup/navigation have meaningful code and VM-report evidence, but accessibility and dialog/keyboard automation are not production-proven. Highest risk: the GUI automation path is internally inconsistent and likely skips/fails in this checkout because the FlaUI harness directory is absent and the scheduled-task runner omits the documented required /IT interactive flag.
proven: Main WPF shell defines a root AutomationId, Segoe UI font, dark palette, disabled-text brush, and button FocusVisualStyle in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:320-424.
Core dashboard/install/help/history/log panels and many controls have x:Name/AutomationId surfaces used by UIA tests in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:497-798 and Tests/FlaUI.Tests.ps1:154-300.

Startup probe mode records popup events and returns pass/fail JSON after the WPF dialog closes in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:1001-1064,3716-3727; Tests/StartupE2E.Tests.ps1:27-90 asserts no startup error popups.
Navigation is implemented by a centralized Show-Panel function with active-nav styling and 150ms fade-in in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:1397-1407,1563-1604.

Dialogs and destructive flows have ESC handlers / cancel paths / confirmations in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:1744-2103,2396-2727,3317-3326; Restore includes explicit data-loss warning in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:1999-2015.
History has filter, refresh, clear controls and an actionable empty state in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:723-745,1319-1393.
An integrated MS01 report documents GUI launch from local, mapped, and path-with-spaces locations plus post-install GUI harness 49/49 passed in Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md:61-80,154-176.
notProven: Current-session GUI execution, screenshots, keyboard-only walkthrough, screen-reader walkthrough, and real WPF focus traversal were not observed; this subagent was read-only and did not launch the GUI.

FlaUI.Tests.ps1 is not runnable from this checkout as-is: it requires Tests\FlaUITestHarness\FlaUITestHarness.psm1, but file lookup found only Tests/FlaUI.Tests.ps1 for FlaUI-named files; Run-GuiTests.ps1 only installs if Tests\FlaUITestHarness\Install-FlaUI.ps1 exists.
Self-hosted GUI CI is not proven reliable: docs say /IT is mandatory for desktop UIA, but Tests/Run-GuiTests.ps1:39-41 creates/runs schtasks without /IT.

Settings and dialog open/close stability is not proven by the production GUI harness; Tests/GuiFullTest.ps1:298-305 explicitly skips Settings dialog interaction because closing can corrupt/instabilize WPF UIA.

Accessibility basics beyond button focus rings are not proven: no AutomationProperties.Name/LabeledBy/HelpText were found for inputs/dialog controls, and only the install panel has explicit TabIndex in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:623-635.

Production-like WSUS/IIS/SQL/WPF desktop behavior is only supported by the MS01 report; the referenced artifacts such as startup-probe.json and gui-fulltest-postinstall.txt are not present in the repository.

findings.title: GUI automation pipeline is inconsistent and not self-contained
findings.evidence: Tests/FlaUI.Tests.ps1:49-59 skips when Tests\FlaUITestHarness is missing or assemblies do not load. File lookup found no Tests/FlaUITestHarness directory, only Tests/FlaUI.Tests.ps1. Tests/Run-GuiTests.ps1:14-18 only installs FlaUI if Tests\FlaUITestHarness\Install-FlaUI.ps1 exists. docs/GUI-TESTING-LESSONS.md:13-27 says scheduled tasks must use /IT and an active desktop session, but Tests/Run-GuiTests.ps1:39-41 creates/runs the scheduled task without /IT.
findings.risk: Release validation can silently skip or fail GUI automation, leaving WPF startup, navigation, dialogs, and keyboard behavior unverified before ship.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Commit the FlaUI harness/install files or remove the dependency; make Run-GuiTests use the documented interactive-session mechanism (/IT with explicit interactive user or an equivalent proven desktop runner); publish fresh flaui-test-results.txt and screenshots/artifacts from the self-hosted runner.
findings.recommendedTests: Run .\Tests\Run-GuiTests.ps1 on the target self-hosted Windows runner with an active desktop session; assert tests are discovered/executed, not skipped, and archive Tests/flaui-test-results.txt plus Tests/Screenshots/.

findings.title: Accessibility is only partially implemented; screen-reader/input labeling is not proven
findings.evidence: Button focus visual exists in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:343-424. Install input TabIndex exists only for InstallPathBox, Browse, password fields, and Install WSUS at Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:623-635. Search found no AutomationProperties.Name, LabeledBy, HelpText, IsDefault, IsCancel, or KeyboardNavigation usage in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1/Modules/WsusDialogs.psm1 except these TabIndex entries. Programmatic dialog helpers set AutomationId but not accessible names/labels in Modules/WsusDialogs.psm1:127-133,204-205,352-353,396-397.
findings.risk: Keyboard users get some button focus affordance, but screen-reader users may encounter unnamed text/password boxes, browse buttons without context, and dialogs without programmatically associated labels. This is a ship readiness gap for accessibility basics.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Add AutomationProperties.Name or LabeledBy for every input, PasswordBox, ComboBox, Image, and ambiguous Browse/Cancel/Save button; add FocusVisualStyle/keyboard navigation to programmatic dialog controls; prove with Inspect.exe/Narrator/UIA dump showing useful Name, ControlType, IsKeyboardFocusable, and tab order.
findings.recommendedTests: Add an accessibility smoke test that launches the GUI, enumerates focusable UIA elements, fails empty Name on controls that require labels, and walks Tab/Shift+Tab order through Install, Settings, Schedule, Maintenance, Restore, and Transfer.

findings.title: Dialog behavior is not production-proven despite code coverage for ESC and confirmations
findings.evidence: Dialog code sets owners/ESC close in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:1747-1755,1854-1862,2108-2116,2631-2635,2797-2810 and New-WsusDialog in Modules/WsusDialogs.psm1:108-133. Destructive confirmations exist at Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:2087-2088,3317-3326. But Tests/GuiFullTest.ps1:199-210 only checks dialog button state, and Tests/GuiFullTest.ps1:298-305 explicitly skips Settings dialog open/close due WPF instability. docs/GUI-TESTING-LESSONS.md:432-449 documents dialog interactions corrupting the UIA tree.
findings.risk: Users may hit modal focus/close regressions, dialog validation dead ends, or post-dialog UIA instability that current automation intentionally avoids.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Stabilize dialog open/close handling or isolate tests; run every modal through open, first focus, validation failure, ESC, Cancel, and successful close in an interactive RDP session; include Settings, Schedule, Maintenance, Restore, Transfer, Export, Import.
findings.recommendedTests: Add ordered dialog E2E tests that reopen the main window after each modal close and verify a dashboard child AutomationId remains findable; run with UIA state-only tests first and destructive dialog tests last.

findings.title: Keyboard shortcut proof is weak and conflicts with documented UIA constraints
findings.evidence: Keyboard shortcuts are implemented in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:824-843. Tests/FlaUI.Tests.ps1:486-533 uses SendKeys for Ctrl+H/Ctrl+R/F5. docs/GUI-TESTING-LESSONS.md:102-103 says SendKeys is unreliable from scheduled tasks, and docs/GUI-TESTING-LESSONS.md:432-439 says SendKeys can hang indefinitely in scheduled-task context. Tests/GuiFullTest.ps1:7-12 says SendKeys was removed from the production GUI test for this reason.
findings.risk: Shortcut behavior may work manually but is not reliably covered in automated release validation; failures in keyboard access could ship undetected.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Either test shortcuts in a supported local interactive harness with raw input injection or replace shortcut automation with direct WPF/UIA invocation plus one manual signed-off keyboard walkthrough.
findings.recommendedTests: Manual or automated interactive desktop test: Tab to main window, invoke Ctrl+D, Ctrl+S, Ctrl+H, Ctrl+R, F5; assert expected panel/status changes and no hangs. Archive result evidence.

findings.title: Long-running operation progress remains mostly log/status based
findings.evidence: Startup splash has progress in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:879-985. Operation runner sets StatusLabel, shows Cancel, disables controls, streams stdout/stderr, and times out in Modules/WsusOperationRunner.psm1:350-430,500-610. Main GUI has InstallProgress but no observed update path in searched progress handlers; long operations enter live-terminal or log-output modes in Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:3108-3133.
findings.risk: For 30+ minute cleanup/sync/install flows, users may see logs but no determinate progress/ETA; this is acceptable for admin tools only if logs are continuous and timeout/cancel behavior is proven in production.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Either define this as the intentional UX and prove continuous log/status/cancel behavior across install, sync, cleanup, restore, reset; or parse progress/steps from CLI output into a visible progress/ETA surface.
findings.recommendedTests: Run one long operation in embedded mode and one in live-terminal mode on a WSUS VM; verify status changes to Running, Cancel appears, controls disable, output updates at least every expected interval, timeout/cancel recover to Ready.

summary: Workflow surface is broad and mostly wired: GUI/CLI operation plans exist for install, restore, transfer, maintenance/sync, schedule, cleanup, diagnostics, reset, health/dashboard, history/notification; standalone scripts exist for GPO, HTTPS, and client check-in. Evidence is mostly static/unit-level. Production-like WSUS/IIS/SQL Express/GPMC/WPF runtime proof is blocked by local environment; restore, cleanup, sync, reset, HTTPS, GPO, SQL login repair, and client check-in must be proven on representative hosts before ship. Two concrete workflow risks stand out: selected-products maintenance is additive despite parameter docs promising exclusive selection, and restore continues after sqlcmd failures without checking exit codes.
proven: Operation-plan builders exist for cleanup/diagnostics/reset/restore, install, maintenance, schedule, and transfer; unit tests cover command shape and secret env use. Evidence: Modules/WsusOperationPlan.psm1:53-172; Tests/WsusArchitectureInterfaces.Tests.ps1:82-124.

History persistence and GUI completion callbacks are unit-tested: JSON history create/prepend/filter/corrupt handling, notification/history/cleanup callbacks. Evidence: Modules/WsusHistory.psm1:19-175; Modules/WsusGuiShell.psm1:396-448; Tests/WsusHistory.Tests.ps1:63-295; Tests/WsusArchitectureInterfaces.Tests.ps1:208-226.

Health-score implementation and dashboard view model exist with unit-level shape/grade tests. Evidence: Modules/WsusHealth.psm1:1468-1615; Modules/WsusGuiShell.psm1:450-512; Tests/WsusHealth.Tests.ps1:416-526; Tests/WsusArchitectureInterfaces.Tests.ps1:231-255.

Scheduled-task module validates inputs and registers tasks with SYSTEM/service-account or user/password paths; mocked tests cover exports, return shape, validation. Evidence: Modules/WsusScheduledTask.psm1:96-342; Tests/WsusScheduledTask.Tests.ps1:1-80,151-220.
notProven: No observed production execution against real WSUS/IIS/SQL Express/GPMC/WPF desktop in this review. Prior local evidence says WSUS/IIS/SQL production stack is absent; full Pester run was aborted after discovering 785 tests and WsusAutoDetection touched real services.

Install, restore, maintenance/sync, cleanup, reset, HTTPS, GPO, SQL login repair, and client check-in workflows are not production-proven from local execution; they require representative host proof with actual services, database, certificates, GPO, and Windows Update client behavior.
GUI E2E beyond element existence is not proven here. FlaUI/GuiFullTest evidence found health/dashboard/control existence, not successful completion of destructive or infrastructure workflows.

findings.title: Production workflow E2E proof is blocked by missing WSUS/IIS/SQL/GPMC/WPF runtime
findings.evidence: Runtime workflows call production dependencies: install uses Install-WindowsFeature, wsusutil postinstall, SQL role changes, IIS/firewall setup (Scripts/Install-WsusWithSqlExpress.ps1:551-766,769-868,994-1028); restore/cleanup/reset call sqlcmd, UpdateServices, wsusutil and services (Scripts/Invoke-WsusManagement.ps1:477-580,1657-1805,1868-1888); HTTPS calls WebAdministration, IIS bindings, wsusutil configuressl (Scripts/Set-WsusHttps.ps1:171-240,392-435); GPO requires DC/GPMC/AD cmdlets (DomainController/Set-WsusGroupPolicy.ps1:1-80,121-220). Prior context: local machine lacks real WSUS/IIS/SQL Express production stack; full Pester attempt was aborted.
findings.risk: Static/unit tests can prove command shape, not that real servers install, sync, restore, reset, bind certs, import GPOs, or check clients in without data loss/outage.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Run documented acceptance suite on representative WSUS server, domain controller, and client: fresh install, optional HTTPS, sync/product-filter, backup/export/import/restore, reset, cleanup, scheduled task run, GPO link/update, SQL login repair, client check-in, GUI operation completion, and dashboard/history verification.
findings.recommendedTests: Infrastructure E2E with snapshots: Windows Server WSUS+SQL Express VM, DC VM with GPMC, client VM. Capture commands, logs, service states, SUSDB state, IIS binding, GPO registry results, client Last Status Report, and GUI screenshots/FlaUI operation completion.

findings.title: Restore workflow does not check sqlcmd exit codes before continuing
findings.evidence: Invoke-WsusRestore runs three sqlcmd calls for SINGLE_USER, RESTORE DATABASE, and MULTI_USER, then starts services, runs wsusutil postinstall/reset without checking $LASTEXITCODE or aborting on restore failure. Evidence: Scripts/Invoke-WsusManagement.ps1:549-580. Restore tests found only backup resolution/integrity, not actual restore failure behavior: Tests/WsusProvisioning.Tests.ps1:30-45; Tests/WsusDatabase.Tests.ps1:364-399.
findings.risk: A failed or partial restore could be followed by service restart/postinstall/reset, obscuring the real failure and leaving SUSDB/service state inconsistent.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Check every sqlcmd exit code/output; abort immediately on failure; always attempt MULTI_USER recovery in a finally block; surface failure to GUI/history. Then prove with a bad backup and a good backup on SQL Express.
findings.recommendedTests: Unit-test sqlcmd wrapper exit handling with simulated nonzero exits; integration-test restore on disposable SUSDB with valid .bak and corrupt/missing .bak, asserting no postinstall/reset after failed restore.

findings.title: SelectedProducts maintenance is additive despite parameter contract implying exclusive product selection
findings.evidence: Parameter help says SelectedProducts 'enables only these in WSUS, disables others' (Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:108-109). Implementation adds selected categories to existing subscription and explicitly says 'Additive mode: add selected products to existing subscription (don't replace)' (Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:735-773). Approval filter later limits approvals to selected ProductTitles (Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:1095-1100), but sync subscription may still include prior products. Tests cover regex/pattern behavior only, not subscription replacement: Tests/ProductFilter.Tests.ps1:23-83,217-260.
findings.risk: Admins using product filter may believe only selected products sync, while stale existing categories remain enabled and can continue expanding metadata/content scope.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Choose contract: either make SelectedProducts replace subscription categories or update docs/UI copy to 'add selected products and limit auto-approval'. If exclusive, test that unselected existing categories are removed before sync.
findings.recommendedTests: Mock WSUS subscription with existing categories; call product configuration path; assert SetUpdateCategories contains exactly selected products for exclusive mode or all existing+selected for documented additive mode.

findings.title: SQL login repair exists in GUI but is not covered like installer SQL escaping path
findings.evidence: GUI SQL login repair directly interpolates current Windows identity in bracketed CREATE LOGIN/ALTER SERVER ROLE SQL while only apostrophes are escaped for the existence check: Scripts/WsusManagementGui.ps1:3395-3455. ProductFilter SQL injection tests assert the install script uses sqlcmd -v variables, not the GUI repair path: Tests/ProductFilter.Tests.ps1:250-263. Installer path does use sqlcmd variables for current user/sysadmin grant: Scripts/Install-WsusWithSqlExpress.ps1:675-689.
findings.risk: Unusual account names containing bracket delimiters or other identifier edge cases can fail or produce unsafe SQL; GUI repair is a privileged workflow and should share the hardened installer approach.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Refactor GUI repair to use the same sqlcmd -v variable strategy or a shared helper that escapes SQL identifiers correctly; add tests with apostrophe and closing-bracket account names.
findings.recommendedTests: Unit-test generated SQL/arguments for DOMAIN\O'Brien and DOMAIN\Name]X; integration-test repair on SQL Express under a test local/domain account.

findings.title: Health score hardcodes localhost HTTP WSUS and SQLEXPRESS service assumptions
findings.evidence: Get-WsusHealthScore service component always checks MSSQL$SQLEXPRESS, WSUSService, W3SVC; sync recency always calls AdminProxy::GetUpdateServer('localhost',$false,8530), ignoring HTTPS/8531 and any configured server/port. Evidence: Modules/WsusHealth.psm1:1468-1585. Dashboard renders this score when present: Modules/WsusGuiShell.psm1:450-512. Tests validate return shape and grade thresholds, not custom HTTPS/port/instance behavior: Tests/WsusHealth.Tests.ps1:416-526.
findings.risk: HTTPS deployments, non-default ports, remote/downstream modes, or non-SQLEXPRESS naming can show degraded/failed health even when production is healthy.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Thread configured WSUS server, UseSsl, port, and SQL service/instance into health score or document it as localhost SQLEXPRESS-only. Prove with HTTP 8530 and HTTPS 8531 deployments.
findings.recommendedTests: Unit-test AdminProxy parameters and service-name resolution from config; integration-test health score on HTTPS-enabled WSUS and non-default SqlInstance if supported.
