summary: Database/SQL readiness is only partially proven. There is code for SUSDB size/stats, cleanup, index/stat maintenance, backup integrity checks, credential storage, SQL adapter/fallback, health diagnostics, and one VM report showing SQL Express + WSUS install plus local staged restore/import. But core production readiness is blocked by unproven real air-gap restore, weak restore failure handling, an invalid-looking monthly deep-cleanup SQL string, no transaction/rollback model for destructive SUSDB maintenance, partial credential/fallback coverage, and no local production-like SQL/SUSDB proof in this review environment.
proven: Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1 exports size/stats, supersession cleanup, index/stat maintenance, shrink, backup integrity, disk-space, and CHECKDB helpers (Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:50-815).
Modules/WsusUtilities.psm1 implements Invoke-WsusSqlcmd with Invoke-Sqlcmd first and sqlcmd.exe fallback, plus QueryTimeout/Credential/Variable parameters (Modules/WsusUtilities.psm1:408-552).

Modules/WsusHealth.psm1 checks SQL service state and SUSDB existence through Invoke-WsusHostSqlQuery, and deep diagnostics query SUSDB download/content tables (Modules/WsusHealth.psm1:144-190, 794-816).

Tests are mostly mocked/syntax/interface coverage: WsusDatabase tests mock Invoke-WsusSqlcmd and cover return shapes/error defaults (Tests/WsusDatabase.Tests.ps1:61-491); WsusHealth tests verify host SQL adapter invocation (Tests/WsusHealth.Tests.ps1:106-155). Prior local evidence: broad baseline subset passed 574/574 with 72 skipped.

Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md reports VM proof for full SQL Express + WSUS install, services running, and restore/import against a locally generated SUSDB_TEST.bak (Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md:1-27, 67-75, 164-176, 425-447).
notProven: No production-like external air-gap .bak + WsusContent restore/import was proven; the VM report explicitly says restore/import used a locally generated export set and real upstream production restore remains unproven (Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md:425-447).

No observed proof that sqlcmd.exe fallback returns objects compatible with call sites expecting properties such as SizeGB, DeletedSuperseded, IndexesRebuilt; tests do not cover Invoke-WsusSqlcmd fallback (Modules/WsusUtilities.psm1:530-552; Tests/WsusUtilities.Tests.ps1:1-260).

No migration/version compatibility layer for WSUS-owned SUSDB schema; code directly queries/deletes internal WSUS tables/procs without checking WSUS/SUSDB schema version (Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:87-205; Scripts/Invoke-WsusManagement.ps1:1763-1798).

No transaction/rollback proof for multi-step destructive cleanup; cleanup catches individual failures and continues, and restore does not check each sqlcmd exit code before starting services/postinstall (Scripts/Invoke-WsusManagement.ps1:552-568, 1747-1841).

No local proof of SQL Express 10GB-limit behavior under sync/cleanup/backup pressure; monitoring exists, but no guard or integration test proves safe behavior near the limit (Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:1394-1464; Modules/WsusAutoDetection.psm1:111-168).

findings.title: Restore path does not verify backup or enforce restore success before continuing
findings.evidence: Invoke-WsusRestore resolves a .bak and escapes single quotes, then runs three sqlcmd.exe commands for SINGLE_USER, RESTORE WITH REPLACE, and MULTI_USER, but does not check $LASTEXITCODE after any command and proceeds to start services, run wsusutil postinstall, and reset content (Scripts/Invoke-WsusManagement.ps1:478-568). Test-WsusBackupIntegrity exists with RESTORE HEADERONLY/VERIFYONLY WITH CHECKSUM, but restore does not call it (Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:527-590).
findings.risk: A failed or partial restore can be reported as complete, services can restart against a bad or unchanged SUSDB, and SINGLE_USER/MULTI_USER recovery is best-effort rather than guaranteed. This is core data-loss/recovery behavior.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Use the shared SQL adapter or checked sqlcmd wrapper, verify selected backup with RESTORE HEADERONLY/VERIFYONLY before RESTORE, stop on nonzero exit/error, wrap service state restoration in finally, and only emit success after observed RESTORE success plus post-restore health check.
findings.recommendedTests: Integration test on SQL Express/WSUS VM: corrupt .bak fails before service changes; valid .bak restore returns nonzero failure if path invalid; valid restore proves SUSDB online, MULTI_USER, WSUS API reachable, and wsusutil reset completed.

findings.title: Monthly maintenance deep cleanup query contains embedded PowerShell text inside the SQL here-string
findings.evidence: $deepCleanupQuery starts at Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:1187, then includes literal lines `# Additional deep cleanup...`, `Write-Log ...`, and a nested `$deepCleanupQuery = @'` before the actual T-SQL body (Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:1187-1214). The query is passed to Invoke-WsusSqlcmd at Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:1217-1223; syntax tests pass because this is inside a PowerShell here-string (Tests/Integration.Tests.ps1:53-60).
findings.risk: Monthly cleanup can hit a SQL syntax error during deep cleanup, log only a warning, and continue as if maintenance succeeded. [INFERENCE] SQL Server is unlikely to accept the embedded PowerShell lines as valid T-SQL.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Remove duplicated PowerShell text from the here-string; make the deep cleanup query a clean T-SQL batch; fail or mark the cleanup phase degraded when this query fails.
findings.recommendedTests: Mock test asserting the exact query text contains no `Write-Log`, `$deepCleanupQuery`, or PowerShell comment lines; VM integration test running Cleanup against SUSDB and asserting StatusRecordsDeleted/TotalOldDeclined or an intentional zero-result success.

findings.title: SQL adapter fallback is not object-compatible with database call sites
findings.evidence: Invoke-WsusSqlcmd returns Invoke-Sqlcmd objects when the module path is available, but returns raw sqlcmd.exe output lines from fallback (Modules/WsusUtilities.psm1:508-552). Call sites access object properties: `$result.SizeGB` (Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:68-69), `$result.DeletedSuperseded` (Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:203-209), `$result.IndexesRebuilt`/`IndexesReorganized` (Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:309-316). WsusUtilities tests do not exercise Invoke-WsusSqlcmd or fallback (Tests/WsusUtilities.Tests.ps1:1-260).
findings.risk: On machines without SQLPS/SqlServer module, SQL commands may execute successfully but callers read null properties, return 0/null, hide failures, or misreport DB size/cleanup counts.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Normalize sqlcmd.exe fallback output into the same object shape as Invoke-Sqlcmd for single-row SELECTs, or require SqlServer module for object-returning functions and fail loudly. Add TrustServerCertificate/ODBC 18 handling consistently for fallback.
findings.recommendedTests: Force fallback path with mocked sqlcmd.exe output for SizeGB, DeletedSuperseded, IndexesRebuilt; assert call sites receive typed properties and nonzero values. Add failure-path tests for missing SqlServer module and missing sqlcmd.exe.

findings.title: Destructive SUSDB maintenance lacks transaction/consistency gates and masks partial failures
findings.evidence: Cleanup deletes from WSUS internal tables and calls spDeleteUpdate one update at a time, suppressing expected errors and continuing (Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:116-205; Scripts/Invoke-WsusManagement.ps1:1747-1810; Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:1293-1356). Index/shrink/stat functions catch errors and return zero/false rather than making the operation fail (Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:309-324, 397-402, 466-486). CHECKDB helper exists but is not wired before/after cleanup (Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:696-794).
findings.risk: Partial cleanup can leave inconsistent maintenance outcomes while the UI/CLI reports progress. Raw deletes against WSUS-owned tables and DBCC SHRINKDATABASE can create operational risk without pre/post consistency proof.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Define a transactional/safe-unit strategy per destructive batch, run pre/post DBCC CHECKDB or physical-only checks where practical, fail the phase on unexpected SQL errors, and document which WSUS internal operations are Microsoft-supported.
findings.recommendedTests: VM integration test with seeded declined/superseded updates: assert cleanup count changes, no CHECKDB errors, WSUS API remains usable, services restart, and repeated cleanup is idempotent.

findings.title: SQL credential support is inconsistent across maintenance operations
findings.evidence: MonthlyMaintenance loads stored SqlCredential for unattended mode (Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:224-247) and passes it to some direct Invoke-WsusSqlcmd calls (Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:1217-1223, 1328-1334, 1399-1406). But WsusDatabase functions invoked by maintenance have no Credential parameter, so Remove-DeclinedSupersessionRecords, Remove-SupersededSupersessionRecords, Optimize-WsusIndexes, Update-WsusStatistics, and Invoke-WsusDatabaseShrink use Windows auth (Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:116-205, 397-399, 466-469; Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:1259-1263, 1293-1296, 1354-1356). Preflight sysadmin check also uses current-user auth only (Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:386-397).
findings.risk: Scheduled/unattended maintenance can pass credentials for backup but fail cleanup/index/shrink/preflight when the task account lacks SQL rights, even if the supplied SQL credential is valid.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Thread Credential through WsusDatabase public functions and preflight checks, or explicitly require the scheduled task identity to be SQL sysadmin and reject SqlCredential for unsupported operations.
findings.recommendedTests: Unit tests asserting Credential is forwarded by each WsusDatabase operation; integration test running unattended maintenance under a non-sysadmin task account with valid SQL credential.

findings.title: Backup workflow is implemented but lacks integrity verification and safe path handling parity
findings.evidence: Monthly backup writes `BACKUP DATABASE SUSDB TO DISK=N'$backupFile' WITH INIT, STATS=10` and then checks the created file size, but does not escape single quotes, use CHECKSUM, run RESTORE VERIFYONLY, or call Test-WsusBackupIntegrity (Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:1377-1418). Old backups are deleted after backup flow based on 90-day retention (Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:1433-1450). VM report shows a manually generated test backup command and local staged restore, not the full production backup verification path (Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md:306-309, 379-381, 425-447).
findings.risk: A backup may be created but unverified/corrupt, backup path quoting can break on unusual valid paths, and retention deletion can proceed without a known-good current backup proof.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Escape backup path or use SQLCMD variables, add WITH CHECKSUM, call RESTORE VERIFYONLY after backup, and gate retention deletion on a verified current backup.
findings.recommendedTests: VM SQL Express test for backup path containing apostrophe; backup verification failure with intentionally corrupt file; retention test ensuring old backups are not deleted when current backup fails verification.

findings.title: SQL Express 10GB limit is monitored but not enforced in write-heavy workflows
findings.evidence: Monitoring exists: database size queries (Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:50-69), dashboard/health thresholds at 7/9/10GB (Modules/WsusAutoDetection.psm1:111-168; Modules/WsusGuiShell.psm1:477-481), and MonthlyMaintenance warns at >=9GB (Scripts/Invoke-WsusMonthlyMaintenance.ps1:1461-1464). But sync/approval/backup/cleanup workflows do not block or alter behavior near the SQL Express cap; Get-WsusDatabaseSize returns 0 on query failure (Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:68-72).
findings.risk: The app can continue sync/approval or report misleading size when SQL queries fail, letting SUSDB hit SQL Express limits during production maintenance.
findings.requiredFixOrProof: Add explicit preflight gates for high DB size/query failure before sync/approval, require cleanup or operator override near 9GB, and treat size-query failure as unknown/degraded rather than 0.
findings.recommendedTests: Mocked and VM tests for size 8.5GB, 9.5GB, query failure, and post-cleanup size reduction; assert workflow warnings/blocks match policy.

featureMatrix.expected: WSUS-owned SUSDB schema managed by wsusutil/postinstall, with app-owned SQL changes versioned/idempotent and compatibility-checked.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: SUSDB creation is delegated to WSUS postinstall; app adds two performance indexes with IF NOT EXISTS and otherwise directly queries/deletes WSUS internal tables.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Code inspection; mocked module tests only. VM report proves WSUS+SQL Express install completed, not schema migration/version compatibility.
featureMatrix.evidence: Install/postinstall path in Scripts/Install-WsusWithSqlExpress.ps1:591-599, 736-745; Add-WsusPerformanceIndexes in Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:339-367; direct internal table use in Modules/WsusDatabase.psm1:87-205.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: No schema version check, migration ledger, or compatibility matrix for WSUS internal table/proc usage.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Document supported WSUS/SUSDB versions and add a preflight compatibility check before destructive SQL.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: VM tests across supported Windows Server/WSUS versions or at least metadata checks proving required tables/procs exist before cleanup.

featureMatrix.expected: Reliable maintenance with accurate results, safe failure semantics, and post-operation consistency proof.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Implemented in WsusDatabase and invoked from management/monthly scripts, but destructive steps catch/continue and lack transactions/CHECKDB gates.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Mocked Pester for return shapes and error defaults; one VM report for broad restore/import/install, not detailed cleanup correctness.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: No production SUSDB cleanup integration proof; no transaction/rollback model; possible invalid monthly deep-cleanup query.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Run cleanup on realistic SUSDB snapshot and prove counts, CHECKDB, WSUS API, and service health after repeated runs.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: Seeded SUSDB VM integration; idempotency test; failure injection for each SQL step.

featureMatrix.expected: Backups are verified before retention deletion; restores verify backup integrity, check every SQL command, restore services safely, and prove WSUS health after restore.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Backup and restore commands exist; Test-WsusBackupIntegrity exists but is not integrated into backup/restore. VM report proves restore against local staged .bak only.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Code inspection plus Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md local staged restore evidence.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: No external air-gap backup set proof; no restore failure tests; no backup checksum/VERIFYONLY in main workflow.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Integrate verification and checked restore, then run valid/corrupt/external-backup VM tests.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: Restore matrix: valid local, valid external, corrupt, missing file, SQL permission failure, service restart failure.

featureMatrix.expected: Single adapter with same output contract under SqlServer module and sqlcmd.exe fallback; supports credentials, timeouts, variables, and trust-server-certificate behavior.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Invoke-WsusSqlcmd centralizes most SQL calls but fallback returns raw text; Invoke-SqlScalar still directly shells to `sqlcmd`; host adapter falls back to Invoke-Sqlcmd.
featureMatrix.testedHow: No observed adapter/fallback tests; WsusHealth tests mock host adapter invocation only.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: Fallback parsing, ODBC 18 trust flag parity, direct Invoke-SqlScalar path, and no-SqlServer-module execution are unproven.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Normalize fallback output or remove fallback for object-returning APIs; add contract tests for both paths.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: Mock both Invoke-Sqlcmd and sqlcmd.exe paths for scalar/single-row/multi-row/error outputs.

featureMatrix.expected: Bounded operations where safe, explicit unlimited timeouts only where necessary, cancellation/failure recovery, and transactions around destructive batches where supported.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Timeout constants exist and many long SQL ops use QueryTimeout 300 or 0; no explicit transactions around multi-step cleanup/restore; restore uses direct sqlcmd without timeout controls.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Code inspection only.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: No cancellation/timeout recovery proof; no transaction boundaries; no proof database returns to MULTI_USER on all restore/repair failures.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Define timeout policy and transaction/finally cleanup per SQL operation; prove failure recovery on VM.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: Failure injection for timeout, SQL deadlock, service stop failure, restore failure after SINGLE_USER.

featureMatrix.expected: Warn and gate dangerous operations as SUSDB approaches 10GB; track trends and prevent silent 0/unknown size from appearing healthy.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: Dashboard/trending/health warnings exist; workflows mostly warn rather than gate.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Code inspection and mocked status tests; no near-limit SQL Express VM proof observed.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: No workflow enforcement near cap; no test for size query failure semantics.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Add policy gates and tests for 7/9/10GB thresholds and unknown size.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: Mocked threshold tests plus large/SUSDB-near-limit VM smoke if feasible.

featureMatrix.expected: Evidence on Windows Server with WSUS/IIS/SQL Express for install, sync, backup, restore, cleanup, diagnostics, and client update lifecycle using real upstream/export data.
featureMatrix.implementationStatus: One VM report covers install, local generated backup/import/restore, diagnostics, access, and registration; local workstation lacks full WSUS/IIS/SQL stack.
featureMatrix.testedHow: Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md plus prior local Pester baseline; no new project-wide tests run in this read-only review.
featureMatrix.evidence: Integrated_GUI_Test_Report_MS01.md:1-27, 67-75, 164-176, 425-447; prior local evidence: 574 passed, 0 failed, 72 skipped, with infrastructure warnings expected locally.
featureMatrix.remainingGaps: External air-gap restore, full upstream sync completion, client download/install, long-run repeated maintenance, and SQL fallback-on-minimal-host remain unproven.
featureMatrix.requiredFixOrProof: Run a production-like acceptance pass on a WSUS/SQL Express VM or lab: real export set, backup verify, restore verify, cleanup, sync completion, client install.
featureMatrix.recommendedCoverage: End-to-end lab script collecting logs, SQL query outputs, WSUS API state, service state, and Event Viewer SQL/WSUS errors before/after each operation.
